description: Narrow, deliberate exception to default.json's 'the hub-served web client gets NO Tauri IPC' rule: grants exactly the start_dragging command to the client's own origin (http://127.0.0.1:<port>, a random port per launch — the client is never served from the tauri:// asset protocol, so it needs an explicit `remote` grant to get ANY IPC at all; core:default alone, scoped only to local app URLs, would not reach it). This is what makes `data-tauri-drag-region` in StatusHeader.svelte's chromeless-window header actually work: Tauri's own injected script invokes plugin:window|start_dragging on a drag-region mousedown, with no Tauri-specific JS in the client itself. Everything else about the client stays exactly as IPC-less as default.json describes — this grants nothing beyond dragging.
